verdict_summary: SSE response fully captured (40262 bytes, 12 chunks, terminator=end_of_stream) via Console fetch+ReadableStream replay. Confirmed Chrome 'Response' tab and HAR export both still empty for SSE — only Console fetch replay (Method c-variant) succeeds.

evidence.method_d_notes: Not attempted — Method (c) succeeded first within budget.
evidence.method_a_notes: DevTools 'Response' tab showed 'Nothing to preview - This request has no response data available' for the original /rest/sse/perplexity_ask request — confirming #743 architectural verdict. EventStream tab DID display 12 parsed events visible in UI (11 'message' + 1 'end_of_stream' with empty data), confirming Chrome internally parses SSE but does not persist raw body for export/copy.

notes: KEY FINDINGS for V7-G perplexity SSE target design: (1) Single event type 'message' for all 11 streaming chunks + 1 terminator 'end_of_stream'. (2) Each chunk is application/json starting with backend_uuid + monotonic cursor. (3) Reliable terminator signals (in order of preference): final_sse_message=true (block-level, on penultimate chunk), status=COMPLETED + message_mode=FULL (penultimate), event:end_of_stream (protocol). (4) Answer text streams in blocks[].markdown_block.chunks where intended_usage='ask_text_0_markdown' — this is the APF injection target. (5) CRLF line endings (\r\n), double-CRLF chunk separator. (6) Authenticated via existing Cookie session (guest mode worked). (7) Body has NO auth tokens — safe to commit raw .txt. Method (c) ReadableStream replay is the only working SSE-aware capture method on Chrome — should be canonical playbook for future SSE-target HAR captures.
